title: Adjust timeline start date based on dependency
excerpt: I’m wanting to adjust the timeline start date based on the end date of a dependency and have found online that I could do this with an adjust command in automations, however this option is not available in my own automat…

posts.author: JennyA (Jenny Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m wanting to adjust the timeline start date based on the end date of a dependency and have found online that I could do this with an adjust command in automations, however this option is not available in my own automation recipes nor available to create from scratch - does anyone know how to achieve such an automation?

image722×80 6.3 KB

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @JennyA

That might be an old article, I don’t believe these automations are available anymore. Instead you can now customize your dependency column and do the setting there. Click the 3-dots menu in the column header and pick Settings > Customize Dependency column.

image655×708 46.5 KB

posts.author: koko03_30 (Konstantin Botev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Jenny,

I think that the TImeline Manipulator app will be pretty helpful in your case. You can check here how it works:

auth.monday.com

Timeline manipulator - monday.com Marketplace

Automations trigger timeline updates when column values change.

Best of luck,
Konstantin
